sniffing, no hardcoded remote-prefix list. The agent-side skill

arXiv / Hugging Face link, or a plain-text idea) and uses whichever
tool fits to bring the content into the experiment folder. Free-text
ideas are saved verbatim to research_source.md and Phase 2 turns
them into a concrete me

fix(backend): add main.py shim for Orbitron auto-detector

Orbitron's docker.js detectPythonEntry looks for backend/main.py and
imports 'from main import app'. Our real app is at backend/app/main.py
so we add a root-level shim that re-exports it.

fix(orbitron): rename Orbitron.yaml -> orbitron.yaml (lowercase)

projectAnalyzer.js looks for lowercase 'orbitron.yaml'. On Linux
(case-sensitive), the capitalized filename was never found, so Orbitron
fell through to auto-detect which mis-read backend/+frontend/ as two
services and failed on env_vars JSONB encoding.

Pivot XAA layer from Okta tenant to xaa.dev playground

The production Okta tenant's XAA audience configuration update is
pending vendor support, so the PoC now targets xaa.dev — Okta's official
XAA playground — which speaks the same three-step protocol
(authorization_code -> token-exchange (ID-JAG) -> jwt-bearer grant).
The legacy Okta path is preserved behind USE_XAA_DEV=false.

- identity/xaa_dev_client.py (new): XAADevClient implementing all three
  xaa.dev POST endpoints with client_secret_

with .js falls through to wildcard catch-all and fails at runtime.

docs: correct plugin-first claim — routing still requires fork patch in both frameworks

The previous commit (4ebd995) claimed ContextEngine was the Hermes plugin seam
for Loom. It isn't — ContextEngine is a compaction abstraction that controls
how messages are shrunk when approaching the token limit, not a routing hook.
After fetching the actual ABC from upstream and reading the plugin system
source, the real picture is:

- Hermes VALID_HOOKS (pre_tool_call, pre_llm_call, on_session_start,
  on

fix: brochure — invoke python3 explicitly to fix "python: not found" in prod

The Python subprocess call in cw-generate-brochure.ts was using `python`,
which failed in the production Debian-slim container with:
  /bin/sh: 1: python: not found

Root cause: the Dockerfile creates a Python venv at /opt/venv and prepends
its bin to PATH. That venv ships `python3` but not always `python`, and
the `/usr/bin/python3 -> /usr/bin/python` symlink earlier in the image
gets shadowed by the venv's bin on PAT

fix(api-server): rotate comma-separated provider API keys (#14346)

### What this PR does

Before this PR:
The local OpenAI-compatible API server (http://127.0.0.1:23333)
forwarded `provider.apiKey` verbatim to the upstream client. For
providers configured with multiple comma-separated keys (e.g.
`key1,key2,key3`), the whole string was sent as the bearer token,
causing upstream 403 `Authorization failed` even though the same
provider worked from the Cherry Studio UI chat path.

fix(release): build workspace packages before MCP bundle (#230)

Stage 2 of release.sh used `npm pack`, which runs `prepack` but NOT
`prepublishOnly` — so the `build:all` topological workspace build wired
into `prepublishOnly` (commit 9349fe9) never ran on release. Result:
`npm run build:mcp` bundled against whatever stale workspace dist/ the
developer happened to have locally.

Fix: call `npm run build:all` explicitly before `build:mcp` in Stage 2
so the release tarball is always built against

fix(brain): unwrap claude-cli type=user/assistant in renderer (#774)

OpenClaw uses type=message with role+content nested in obj.message.
Claude-cli uses type=user/assistant with the same {role, content}
nested under obj.message. The OSS brain renderer only unwrapped the
OpenClaw shape, so any claude-cli-format event flowing through
routes/brain.py would render with role="" and miss the user/assistant
text-extraction branches entirely.

This is a future-proofing change -- the OSS brain reader cu

fix(patcher): eliminate infinite loop on trailing non-prefixed lines

parse_patch had an infinite loop in the UPDATE_FILE_MARKER while block:
when a hunk is followed by a bare context line (e.g. 'greet("world")'),
the line doesn't match any known prefix (+, -, space, ***, @@) so:
  - the outer loop doesn't break (not ***)
  - no context i++ (not @@)
  - the inner hunk while breaks immediately (else: break)
  - hunk is empty, not appended
  - i is unchanged → infinite loop

Fix: track i_before_hu

Run #3 produced a good, grounded report — but gemma achieved it by
spawning a sub-agent and handing the whole task off. The sub-agent ran
in a separate session; the user could not watch the progress in the
foreground chat. Even though the output was correct, the workflow
defeats observability, which is a first-class user requirement for
locally-hosted models.

fix(sandbox): harden proxy-env.sh permissions to prevent sandbox write (#2181) (#2208)

## Summary

Fixes a security bug where `/tmp/nemoclaw-proxy-env.sh` was writable by
the sandbox user (`chmod 644`), allowing a sandbox-resident attacker to
append arbitrary shell code that executes on every `nemoclaw <name>
connect` via `.bashrc`/`.profile` sourcing. Introduces shared helpers to
prevent this class of bug going forward.

## Related Issue

Fixes #2181

Fix upload stream handling to prevent truncated files (#14267)

## Summary
- Replace single `Read()` call in Go upload service with `io.ReadAll()`.
- Prevent potential truncated/corrupted file content during multipart
upload.
- Keep existing API behavior unchanged while fixing data integrity risk.

## Root Cause
`io.Reader.Read()` may return fewer bytes than requested without an
error. The previous implementation read once into a full buffer and
assumed all bytes were populated.

-

feat(cli): add desktop source install command (#546)

## Summary

Adds the official CLI entrypoint for building and installing the Signet Electron desktop app from an existing source checkout.

`signet desktop build` resolves the local checkout, runs `bun install`, and then runs `bun run build:desktop`.

`signet desktop install` uses the same build path, then installs the newest Linux AppImage as a user-level launcher with a desktop entry. This gives Arch Linux a working native install path now

feat: per-session workspace isolation (CMA parity)

Migrate from per-agent to per-session workspace directories. Each session
now gets its own bind mount at <hostStateRoot>/<agentId>/sessions/<sessionId>/
instead of sharing <hostStateRoot>/<agentId>/ across all sessions.

This unlocks:
- Per-session filesystem isolation — session A can't read session B's files
- Concurrent sessions per agent — different workspaces = different Pi locks
- CMA sandbox parity

content: new post — The Tests That Bailed Silently

Draft promoted from blog-seeds-s01-c6944c.md (Apr 20).

Story: five CI test suites had been showing '0 assertions / bailed'
or '3 skipped' for weeks — all pointing at retired repos/cli/.
Everyone treated them as 'known broken' and moved on. Fixing the five
path refs (no test logic changes) took assertions from 800 -> 1041
and surfaced four real user-impacting bugs: migration marker stall,
protocol heat reset on reinstall, skill-loader dropping

fix(ci): install local plugin-sdk in dryrun and desktop builds

修复 Release Dry Run 在 main 上长期失败的根因：所有 8 个 job 都因
`openakita-plugin-sdk>=0.3.0` 在 PyPI 找不到（PyPI 只有 0.1.0/0.2.0，
本地 monorepo 已是 0.6.0）而崩溃。

改动：
- release-dryrun.yml `python_wheel`：增加构建 SDK wheel 步骤，验证
  extras 时用 `--find-links dist/` 让 pip 从本地索引解析 SDK，真实
  模拟"SDK 已先发布到 PyPI、用户随后 pip install openakita[all]"
  的发布完成态（仍走完整版本约束解析，依赖声明错误能立即暴露）。
- release-dryrun.yml `dryrun_build` (7 平台矩阵)：在
  `pip install -e ".[all]"` 之前先 `pip install ./op

fix(ci): publish plugin-sdk before main package on PyPI

main 包的 [plugins]/[all] extras 依赖 openakita-plugin-sdk>=X，
原先发布顺序是先 main 后 SDK，会出现一段 "main 已上架但 SDK 还没
上架" 的窗口期，期间任何 `pip install openakita[all]` 都会因找不到
SDK 版本而失败。

调整为：
1. 先发 dist-sdk/ 到 PyPI
2. 用 pip index versions 轮询确认 SDK 已被 PyPI 索引（最多 5 分钟）
3. 再发 dist/ (main package)

确保用户跑 `pip install openakita==X[all]` 时 SDK 必然可解析。

attacker-injected message had a nanoid id while msg0

directory on disk permanently.

fix(vuln-rules): remove mcpServers field dependency, match on file format only

Detection no longer requires specific field names in the response body.
New matchers rely solely on file format validity:
- JSON config files: body~="^\s*\{" (body starts with '{', rejects HTML 404 pages)
- Goose YAML: body~="(?m)^\w+\s*:" (valid YAML key-value lines)

The MITM proxy's CONNECT handler used to attach an 'error' listener to the
raw client socket only AFTER `await getHostCert(hostname)` and the TLS
wrap. If the client RSTs its TCP connection inside that window — which
happens in long-running setups where clients time out, reload configs,
or retry under load — Node emits an unhandled `read ECONNRESET` on the
raw socket and aborts the fws Node process. Because fws d

ci(canary): consolidate Live Canary to one daily 02:00 UTC slot (#2831)

Previously the hourly staggered schedule (five crons at :00/:15/:30/
:45/:50) kicked off a separate workflow run per cron, which produced
24 runs/day per lane group, four red dots per day when something

spawning while still letting the model delegate aggressively:

- depth <= 2 from the root session (a sub-agent cannot spawn its own

- <= 5 direct children per parent session (concurrent cap)
- <= 20 sub-agents total across the whole session tree

fix(tools): exclude parallel_edit when GPT patch-only mode is active (#11)

parallel_edit delegates to edit.execute() internally, so leaving it
available on gpt- models lets them bypass the usePatch filter that
removes EditTool and WriteTool. Excluding parallel_edit alongside
edit+write when usePatch is true restores the intended single-tool
invariant.

fix(growth): x engagement approve now actually posts the reply (#3340)

The xeng_approve and xeng_edit_submit handlers marked the reply as
approved in state.db but never called postToX(). Replies were silently
stuck in "ready to post on X" limbo forever.

Both handlers now call postToX(replyText, sourceTweetId) so the reply
goes out as an actual threaded reply on X, and the Slack card shows
the live tweet URL. Mirrors the tweet_approve flow.

login, email signup, Google login, Google signup) so attribution no
longer relies solely on the client firing the event. Uses isNewUser

signup_source (self / invite) on signup so we can segment self-serve
vs invited activations.

feat(client/analytics): persist rdt_cid for Reddit Conversions API

Mirrors the gclid flow for Reddit: reads rdt_cid from the URL, persists
it in a 30-day .team9.ai cookie (matches Reddit's attribution window),
and writes both rdt_cid and $initial_rdt_cid to the PostHog person so
the Reddit Conversions API destination can attribute signup_completed
regardless of how the user arrives at signup (direct, homepage hop,
Google OAuth round-trip).

every answer/ICE; new offers always created non-primary PCs because the

Fix large numeric tool args rendered as scientific notation (e.g. 2.004555e+06) (#94)

Go's json.Unmarshal decodes JSON numbers as float64, and fmt.Sprintf("%v")
formats large values with exponents. Use strconv.FormatInt for whole numbers
and strconv.FormatFloat with 'f' verb for fractional values to preserve the
original decimal representation.

fix(ops): raise gunicorn timeout 120s→300s to survive cron spikes

Workers were getting SIGABRT'd every ~5 min when Smart trigger cron +
reflections generation (20 AI calls + sync psycopg2) exceeded the 120s
timeout and blocked the event loop. Kills in-flight requests as 500s
and resets in-memory state (pool, caches) every cycle.

300s gives cron headroom; proper fix is moving cron out of HTTP worker
(tracked separately).

Also adds --graceful-timeout 30 so in-flight requests get a chance to
fin

without timeouts. If claude --version (routing through LiteLLM proxy) or
npm/pip commands hang, the entire cron job blocks and times out after 300s.

Before this PR:

Cherry Studio launches `claude-code` by having Bun execute
`bin/claude.exe`. Since `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` switched to native
binary distribution, `bin/claude.exe` is now a Mach-O/ELF/PE native
binary (or a shell placeholder if postinstall didn't run). Bun parses it
as JavaScript and fails on all platforms:
- **macOS/Linux**: `error: Expected ";" but found ...` (Bun treat

in the container (the real key was returned instead of the test key).

only the API key always uses the passed argument.

test_key_injected_from_argument,

fix(kernel): fork inherits parent's SessionInterrupt for external cancel (#2945)

Closes #2939. `run_forked_agent_streaming` previously allocated a fresh
`SessionInterrupt` per fork, so `stop_agent_run(parent)` had no way to
cancel tools running inside a fork turn — the parent's cancel signal
never reached the fork's tool-polling futures.

`SessionInterrupt` already wraps `Arc<AtomicBool>` and is `Clone`, so
fixing this is just a matter of handing the fork a clone of the parent's
interrupt when

fix queue settlement clobbering status update

`settleQueueAfterTerminalStatus` previously returned a whole Thread,
which the caller then passed to `updateThreadState` as a full
replacement. That clobbered the `status: idle`/`error` update
scheduled moments earlier by `applyIpcEvent` — the Thread snapshot
read from a ref that hadn't caught up, so the stale "running" status
would land on top of the fresh terminal one and the "Thinking…"
indicator would stick forever.

[Sprint 5] TEMPLATE-* UDS verbs (#129)

Adds three new AIMX/1 verbs — TEMPLATE-CREATE, TEMPLATE-UPDATE, and
TEMPLATE-DELETE — so unprivileged callers can register, revise, and
remove hook templates bound to their own Linux user. Together they
let `aimx agent-setup` (Sprint 6) drop its "rerun sudo aimx setup"
step while keeping operator-only escape hatches for reserved run_as
values and raw cmd hooks.

- S5-1 wire protocol: new Request variants (TemplateCreate / Update /
  Delete), Template-Name

Skip gateway directory fsync on Windows to stabilize CI runtime state writes

Windows runners reject opening temp directories for the parent-directory
fsync step used by gateway state persistence. The gateway integration
suite then loses status and stop-request updates, which cascades into
control-surface timeouts only on the Windows default test lane.

This keeps the existing fsync behavior on non-Windows platforms and
fails open on Windows where the directory sync primitive is not portable.

Kimi's /coding endpoint speaks the Anthropic Messages protocol but has
its own thinking semantics: when thinking.enabled is sent, Kimi validates
the history and requires every prior assistant tool-call message to carry
OpenAI-style reasoning_content. The Anthropic path never populates that
field, and convert_messages_to_anthropic strips Anthropic thinking blocks
on third-party endpoints — so after one tool-calling turn the
refactor(smartcmp): tighten request workflow contracts

fix(ux): add 'spawn last' to reconnect hints in cloud modules (#3337)

The reconnect hints shown after provisioning in all 5 cloud providers
(Hetzner, AWS, DigitalOcean, GCP, Sprite) only showed raw SSH/CLI
commands. Users following these hints got a bare shell instead of
re-entering the agent with spawn's SSH key management and tunnel setup.

Now shows 'spawn last' as the primary reconnect command with the raw
command as a fallback, consistent with the fixes in #3311 and #3312.

writing them straight into `openclaw.json`. That made it easy to create
config keys that OpenClaw does not recognize, which then breaks agent
startup.

This change makes `config set` reject unrecognized config paths before
it writes anything.

fix(m31_001): add startTimeUnixNano to OTLP data points (greptile P1)

Greptile flagged aggregationTemporality:2 (CUMULATIVE) paired with
per-bucket-delta counts as semantically inconsistent. On analysis:

- Prometheus histograms are cumulative-from-process-start counters — every
  scrape returns the running total, never a per-interval delta. The correct
  OTLP encoding for that source is CUMULATIVE.
- The per-bucket-delta conversion (cumul[i] - cumul[i-1]) is about
  Prometheus-cumulative-acros

ci(test-integration): use us-west registry mirror to avoid GitHub runner timeouts

GitHub-hosted runners (Azure eastus) frequently time out when pulling
base image metadata from `higress-registry.cn-hangzhou.cr.aliyuncs.com`,
causing `make build-hiclaw-controller` (and other docker builds) to fail

fix(dev): postgres:18-alpine PGDATA path + capture Tier 2 green run

The compose image was bumped to postgres:18-alpine previously without the
matching env tweak: 18+ expects /var/lib/postgresql/ as the mount root and
PGDATA pointing at a subdirectory. Mounting to /var/lib/postgresql/data
directly makes Postgres 18 refuse to start ("unused mount/volume" error).

Minimal fix: add PGDATA=/var/lib/postgresql/data/pgdata so Postgres creates
a subdir under the existing mount; no compose restructure n

feat: persist full exception detail to runtime_error + WS + console

Agent loop failures previously wrote only the generic "Agent loop
failed" string to t_session.runtime_error and the sessionStatus WS
payload, so neither the DB row nor the browser could show the
underlying cause. Only the server log (via SLF4J + throwable) had it,
which is painful to search after the fact.

This change adds StackTraceFormatter to produce a compact, bounded
text rendering of a Throwable plus its Caused-by chain

fix(github): preserve reasoning_effort for non-Claude models (#713)

The previous blanket strip in GithubExecutor.transformRequest removed
`thinking` AND `reasoning_effort` for every GitHub-routed model to avoid
Claude-on-Copilot 400s from OpenClaw. That regressed GPT-5 family support
(gh/gpt-5-mini honors reasoning_effort: low/medium/high).

Make supportsThinking(model) model-aware — return false only for Claude
models, so the strip fires only where the upstream actually rejects these
fields.

they render as literal text, which is confusing for users.

* fix(skills/baoyu-comic): require absolute paths for curl -o downloads

When downloading generated images across several batches of image_generate
calls, relying on persistent-shell CWD is unsafe. The terminal tool's shell
can rotate (TERMINAL_LIFETIME_SECONDS expiry, a failed cd that leaves the
shell somewhere else), and 'curl -fsSL <url> -o relative.png' then silently
writes to the wrong directory with no error.

* fix(migrate): v0_13_0 shells out to `gbrain` shim, not `process.execPath`

On bun-installed trees, process.execPath is the bun runtime itself.
`bun extract links ...` got reinterpreted as `bun run extract` and

feat(runtime): add crash handler with guest console ring buffer

Installs a Windows SEH/MiniDumpWriteDump-based crash handler in the VM
runtime so hard faults (access violations, stack overflows, WHPX-level
aborts) produce a usable .dmp next to the VM's runtime.log instead of
silently terminating. A lock-free ring buffer captures the last N bytes
of guest console output so a dump can be correlated with the kernel
panic or early-boot trace that triggered it.

- runtime/crash_handler.{h,cpp}: Inst

feat(whpx): add Windows 10 1803 soft-APIC fallback and hi-res LAPIC timer

WHPX on Windows 10 1803 lacks WHvRequestInterrupt, the partition-level
LocalApicEmulationMode property, and the notification-port APIs. Previous
builds produced a loader error ("entry point WHvRequestInterrupt not
found") on 1803 hosts and, even where exports resolved, hung in guest
SMP bring-up or faulted user-space init because a large set of CPUID
features were passed through that WHPX does not actually virtualise.

## Summary
Bumps the OpenShell version range from `0.0.26` → `0.0.29` so fresh
NemoClaw installs pull a build that actually enforces the Landlock
filesystem policy on the sandbox process.

## Root cause recap
OpenShell 0.0.26's Landlock setup ran *after* `drop_privileges()`, so
path-fd opens failed as the unprivileged sandbox user and the
`best_effort` handler silently returned success. The sandbox then ran
unco

[Sprint 2] Per-mailbox storage ownership (#126)

Every daemon write path now chowns its output to the mailbox's configured
owner with mode 0600 (files) or 0700 (directories). This enforces the
PRD §7.1 isolation invariant end-to-end: alice reads her own inbox, bob
gets EACCES on alice's.

- MAILBOX-CREATE verb gains required Owner: header; handler creates
  inbox/<name> + sent/<name>, chowns, chmods 0700; rollback only removes
  dirs the handler itself created
- ingest.rs chowns new .md, bundle

fix(tui): don't swallow Kimi/Qwen ~! ~? kaomoji as subscript spans

The inline markdown regex had `~([^~\s][^~]*?)~` for Pandoc-style subscript
(H~2~O, CO~2~). On models that decorate prose with kaomoji like `thing ~!`
and `cool ~?` — Kimi especially — the opener `~!` paired with the next
stray `~` on the line and dim-formatted everything between them with a
leading `_` character, mangling markdown output.

Tighten the pattern to short alphanumeric-only content (`~[A-Za-z0-9]{1,8}~`)

## Summary
Reverts #2186 (which made Brev E2E failures 10× slower) and replaces
\`sudo npm link\` with a direct \`sudo ln -sf\` symlink in both the
launchable setup and the in-test bootstrap path. \`npm link\` is
overkill for what we actually need and hangs indefinitely on cold CPU
Brev instances. Direct symlink is O(1) and deterministic.

refactor(cli): replace spawnSync("sleep") with native wait utility (#2027)

<!-- markdownlint-disable MD041 -->
## Summary
This PR refactors the NemoClaw CLI to replace inefficient and
platform-dependent `spawnSync("sleep")` subprocess calls with a native
Node.js `wait` utility. By using `Atomics.wait`, we provide a reliable
synchronous sleep mechanism that avoids the overhead of spawning new
processes, improving performance and robustness during onboarding and
deployment.

own workers; leaf children cannot delegate further (identical to today).

Default posture is flat — max_spawn_depth=1 means a depth-0 parent's
children land at the depth-1 floor and orchestrator role silently
degrades to leaf. Users opt into nested delegation by raising
ma

work in parallel instead of serialising it:

  newString, replaceAll?}` entries and runs the underlying `edit`
  tool for each one concurrently. Per-file successes and failures are
  returned together so a bad hunk does not abort the rest of the
  batch. Only fails the whole call if *every* entry fails.

fix: resolve media handles in upload tools so LLM can use handles across tools (#992)

The LLM sees media handles (media_XXXXXX) in the prompt from analyze_photo
and naturally passes them to upload tools. But companycam_upload_photo,
upload_to_storage, and organize_file only understood original URLs, not
handles. When the LLM passed a handle as original_url, all three lookup
tiers (downloaded_media, staging, MediaStore) missed, and the upload
failed with "No photo available."

Adds resolve_media

fix(session): fsync sessions on graceful shutdown to prevent data loss

On filesystems with write-back caching (rclone VFS, NFS, FUSE mounts)
the OS page cache may buffer recent session writes. If the process is
killed before the cache flushes, the most recent conversation turns are
silently lost — causing the agent to "forget" recent context and
respond to stale history on the next startup.

Changes:

- session/manager.py: add fsync=True option to save() that flushes the
  file and its parent d

chore: refresh narrative demo

Honest doc rewrite: external PostgreSQL is not supported by OpenShell's HTTP-only proxy

Deep research + end-to-end tests against live sandbox confirm:

FLAG (architectural limitation in upstream OpenShell):
OpenShell sandboxes have a single egress route to `10.200.0.1:3128`, an
HTTP CONNECT proxy. Raw-TCP clients (the PostgreSQL wire protocol) cannot
speak HTTP CONNECT, and the sandbox has no SOCKS / transparent TCP
redirect. L4 policy entries (endpoints without `protocol: rest`) apply
to CONNE
